description: The reflexive object pronoun 'miso' says that the subject acts upon itself. The subject supplies person and number, so one form covers every English self-pronoun. Its partner 'wiso' (RECP) carries action from one group member to another instead.
articulatory_notes: Voiced 'm' resonates through the nose behind closed lips, which open into unrounded high 'i'. For 'so', the tongue makes a narrow alveolar groove before rounded lips form an 'o' at true-mid height. Stress rests on 'mi'.
sound_symbolism: Final 'so' is the audible family mark shared with the reciprocal. The hum behind closed lips gives 'miso' the more inward contour of the pair.
usage_notes: In a clause, 'miso' takes the direct object's place ahead of any verb particles. It never supplies the subject. When the subject is plural, the action returns to each acting member; 'wiso' expresses action between members.
